meta.title: F30 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW 3 Series F30 engine — from the 1.6 N13 to the 2.0 N20/B48 and 3.0 N55/B58/N57. Real UK costs, honest reliability data. Compare prices from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 3 Series F30 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned F30 engine costs between £2,500 and £8,000 supply-only, depending on the engine — the N13/N20 four-cylinder petrols range £2,500–£6,000, the B48/B47 units run £2,800–£5,500, and the six-cylinder N55/B58/N57 engines run £4,500–£8,000. Fitting adds 8–16 hours of labour at £65–£90/hr. Use our calculator for a personalised quote.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 3 Series F30 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58B30 3.0-litre six-cylinder (340i) and B48B20 2.0-litre four-cylinder (330i) are the most reliable engines in the F30 range — both address the N20's timing-chain issues and deliver proven BMW modular-engine durability. On diesel, the B47D20 (post-2015) is significantly more reliable than the N47 it replaced.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 3 Series F30?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain guide wear on the N20 petrol (pre-2015 320i/328i), timing chain tensioner failure on the N47 diesel (pre-2015 316d–320d), EGR cooler failure on the B47 diesel (LCI 318d/320d), and cooling system leaks on the B38/B48/B58 petrols. The F30 also suffers from electric power steering vagueness and suspension component wear on high-mileage cars.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 3 Series F30 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends heavily on the engine and model year. Pre-2015 N20 and N47 engines carry the highest risk of timing-chain failure. Post-2015 B-series engines (B38, B48, B58, B47) are significantly more reliable. For any F30, a thorough engine inspection and maintenance history check are essential.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 3 Series F30 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pre-2015 N20 (320i/328i) and N47 (316d–320d) engines without documented timing-chain replacement carry the highest risk. We consistently advise buyers to avoid early 328i and 320d examples without a full timing-chain history. If you must have a pre-2015 car, budget for the chain work immediately.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my F30 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Whining noise when revving (N20 timing chain), cold-start rattle (N47 timing chain), coolant loss or overheating (B47 EGR or B-series cooling system), or blue smoke and rough idle on any high-mileage unit are the key warning signs. Use our diagnostic tool for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 3 Series F30 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 70% BMW scrap threshold. Against a well-kept £10,000 F30, a £3,200 N47 recon is 32% of the car's value — safe. Against a tired £5,000 early example, the same recon is 64% — borderline. For sub-£4,000 examples, we'd recommend walking away.

mainEntity.name: How long do BMW 3 Series F30 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58 and B48 engines can comfortably exceed 150,000 miles with routine maintenance. The B47 diesel is equally durable, provided the EGR cooler recall work is completed. The N20 and N47 are capable of 150,000+ miles but require proactive timing-chain replacement at 60,000–80,000 miles to reach that figure.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the F30 and other 3 Series generations?

acceptedAnswer.text: The F30 (2011–2019) is the sixth-generation 3 Series and the first to be powered exclusively by turbocharged engines — no naturally aspirated options were offered. It replaced the E90 (2005–2011) and was succeeded by the G20 (2019–present). The F30 introduced electric power steering and offered the first plug-in hybrid 3 Series (330e). The F30 also had a more spacious interior and larger boot than the E90.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 3 Series F30 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is listed on the VIN plate, vehicle documentation, and on a sticker under the bonnet or on the engine block. For 1.6 petrol, look for N13B16 (316i). For 2.0 petrol, look for N20B20 (320i/328i) or B48B20 (320i/330i LCI). For 3.0 petrol, look for N55B30 (335i) or B58B30 (340i LCI). For 2.0 diesel, look for N47D20 (pre-LCI) or B47D20 (LCI). For 3.0 diesel, look for N57D30 (330d/335d). Tell us your variant and year, and we'll confirm.

hero.h1: BMW 3 Series F30 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the F30-generation BMW 3 Series — from the 1.6-litre N13 four-cylinder to the 2.0-litre N20 and B48, and the 3.0-litre N55 and B58 six-cylinder turbos. Real UK costs, honest failure data, and the N20 timing-chain question that defines this generation's ownership story. Backed by 380+ real UK F30 enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: BMW 3 Series F30 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the F30 saloon specifically — from the 2011 launch through the 2015 LCI facelift and the introduction of the B-series modular engines.

overview.intro: The BMW F30 is the sixth generation of the BMW 3 Series, produced from October 2011 to June 2019. It marked a significant departure from its predecessor (the E90) by being the first 3 Series generation powered exclusively by turbocharged engines, with no naturally aspirated options available. The F30 also introduced electric power steering, replacing the hydraulic system of the E90. The engine line-up evolved substantially over its production run: early cars used the N13 1.6-litre four, N20 2.0-litre four, and N55 3.0-litre six petrols, alongside N47 2.0-litre and N57 3.0-litre six diesels. The 2015 Life Cycle Impulse (LCI) facelift brought BMW's modular B-series engines — the B38 three-cylinder, B48 four-cylinder, and B58 six-cylinder — replacing the N-series units in most applications. The F30 was succeeded by the G20 3 Series in 2019.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW L7 platform • Predecessor: BMW 3 Series E90 (2005–2011) • Successor: BMW 3 Series G20 (2019–present) • Sibling body styles in this generation: F31 Touring, F34 Gran Turismo, F35 long-wheelbase saloon (China)
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The F30 3 Series generated 380+ real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED], concentrated on the 2.0-litre N47 and N20 engines as the parc exits warranty and timing-chain concerns come to the fore.

items.engine: B47D20 2.0-litre (190 bhp) 320d LCI — The post-2015 B47 diesel addressed the N47's defining weakness — the rear-mounted timing chain. With improved chain tensioner design and a more robust bottom end, the B47 is the diesel to choose in the F30. EGR cooler issues are recall-addressed, and DPF clogging is manageable with regular motorway driving.
items.whoItsFor: high-mileage buyers who want the sweet spot of economy and performance.

items.engine: B48B20 2.0-litre (252 bhp) 330i — The 252 bhp B48 four-cylinder is the sweet spot in the F30 petrol range. It delivers genuine performance — 0-60 in 5.8 seconds — with the economy of a four-cylinder and, crucially, none of the N20's timing-chain anxiety. It's the engine that makes the F30 a genuinely compelling used buy.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want real performance without six-cylinder running costs.

items.engine: N20B20 (pre-2015 328i) — The N20's plastic timing chain guides wear prematurely, leading to chain slack and, in catastrophic cases, engine destruction. The whining noise on revving is the classic warning sign. Pre-March 2015 production cars are the highest risk.
items.whoItsFor: only buyers with documented timing-chain replacement — and a healthy contingency fund.

items.engine: N57D30 (335d) — The six-cylinder diesel's timing chain and turbocharger wear can result in expensive repairs. A full reconditioned replacement runs £4,500–£7,500 — and that's before addressing the associated turbo and fuel-system work that often accompanies high-mileage failures.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want effortless six-cylinder torque and accept the premium.

items.engine: B48B20 2.0-litre (184 bhp) 320i LCI — The post-2015 184 bhp B48 offers the perfect balance of performance, economy, and reliability for the F30. It's the most common F30 petrol, parts are widely available, and the recon cost is significantly lower than the six-cylinder options.
items.whoItsFor: the majority of F30 buyers who want a dependable daily driver.

items.engine: B58B30 3.0-litre (326 bhp) 340i — Often overshadowed by the M3, the 340i's B58 delivers 326 bhp and 450 Nm of torque — enough for 0-60 in 4.8 seconds. It's considered the most reliable modern BMW six-cylinder, with none of the N55's VANOS issues and none of the N20's timing-chain anxiety.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want genuine performance without the M3's running costs.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Risk vs Reward — pre-2015: budget for chain work
Solid Choice — reliable six, but higher running costs
Highest Risk — chain failure is catastrophic

economicsRule.title: The F30 rule
economicsRule.text: A £3,200–£6,000 N20 recon against a £4,000–£8,000 early F30 sits at 40–150% of the car's value. On a sub-£6,000 example, that brushes our 70% BMW threshold — we'll honestly tell you to walk away. Against a well-kept £10,000+ example, the same recon drops to a routine 32–60% — a repair decision, not a financial one. Value the car first.

keyTakeaways.question: Which F30 Costs Least To Own?
Which F30 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: N57 335d — timing chain and turbo repairs add cost.

keyTakeaways.question: Which F30 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: B48 330i LCI — the sweet spot between performance and running costs.

problems.title: N20 Timing Chain Guide Wear
problems.rootCause: The plastic timing chain guides on N20 engines wear prematurely, causing chain slack. A whining noise when revving is the classic warning sign. If ignored, the chain can slip, causing catastrophic engine damage. Pre-March 2015 production cars are the highest risk.
problems.recommendation: On any pre-2015 F30 N20, have the timing chain inspected immediately — a £1,200–£2,500 preventative repair is cheaper than the £3,200–£6,000 replacement that follows if ignored.

cta.label: Compare N20 engine prices for your F30 →

problems.title: N47 Diesel Timing Chain Tensioner Failure
problems.rootCause: The rear-mounted timing chain on N47 diesel engines — a design shared with the E90 — receives the last oil feed and is tensioned by a plastic guide that degrades over time. Cold-start rattle is the classic early warning; ignored, it causes catastrophic engine failure.
problems.recommendation: On any pre-2015 F30 diesel, have the timing chain inspected at 60,000 miles — a £1,000–£2,500 preventative repair is cheaper than the £3,200–£5,500 replacement that follows if ignored.

cta.label: Compare N47 engine prices for your F30 →

problems.title: B47 Diesel EGR Cooler Failure
problems.rootCause: The EGR cooler on B47 diesel engines can develop internal coolant leaks, leading to coolant loss and, in rare cases, fire risk. BMW issued a recall for this issue — any car without documented recall work carries this risk.
problems.recommendation: On any F30 diesel, check the EGR cooler recall status immediately — a £500–£1,200 preventative repair is cheaper than the £2,800–£5,000 replacement that follows if ignored.

cta.label: Compare B47 engine prices for your F30 →

replacementCosts.h2: F30 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are F30 saloon only. F31 Touring, F34 Gran Turismo, and F35 long-wheelbase saloon share most of these engines but are covered on their own Generation Pages. The F30 was the first 3 Series to be offered with a plug-in hybrid (330e) from 2015, and the ActiveHybrid 3 was available from 2012 to 2015. The LCI facelift (2015) introduced the B38, B48, and B58 engines, replacing the N13, N20, and N55 in most applications.
